project_type: Python trading system with MT5 integration, backtest engine, and live trader
build_system: None (plain Python scripts). User runs scripts directly from flat directory on Windows.
test_framework: No formal test framework. Validation is done by running backtest scripts that produce JSON results.
test_command: python3 -c "print('Syntax check passed')" && python3 -m py_compile adaptive_system/core/regime_detector.py && python3 adaptive_system/run_demo.py
verification_instructions: 1. Run python3 adaptive_system/run_demo.py to verify the system works with synthetic data. 2. Verify all Python files compile without errors using py_compile. 3. Check that imports work correctly in flat-layout mode.
setup_instructions: pip install numpy pandas (already available in sandbox). On user's Windows PC: pip install numpy pandas MetaTrader5
environment_constraints: Open internet available. No MT5 terminal (Windows-only). Cannot test live trading connectivity. Must validate with synthetic data and backtest logic only. User runs from flat directory (F:\Automation\EA Testing\HFT\HFT_v2\) so all imports must work without package hierarchy.
contribution_requirements: User downloads files via HFTUpdate.bat which pulls raw files from GitHub. New system goes in HFT_Scalper_Pro/adaptive_system/ directory. Must include its own HFTUpdate section or separate bat file.
key_patterns: Existing system uses: numpy arrays for computation, pandas for data loading, dataclass for configs, signal arrays of shape (n,3) with [direction, sl_dist, tp_dist], BaseStrategy ABC pattern, ATR-based stops, RSI/EMA indicators computed manually (no TA-lib dependency), two-mode position sizing (grow/protect), CONTRACT_SIZE=100, 1:500 leverage, INITIAL_EQUITY=1000
directory_structure: HFT_Scalper_Pro/ contains: hft_scalper/ (existing single-pair system), HFTUpdate.bat (download script). The new system will be at HFT_Scalper_Pro/adaptive_system/ with a flat-import-friendly layout.
